title: Validation, regularization and reproducibility

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Validation, regularization and reproducibility; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.claim: Neural experiments separate training, validation, and test evidence; regularization, early stopping, augmentation, architecture, and seeded repetitions control or measure generalization without tuning on the test.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Neural experiments separate training, validation, and test evidence; regularization, early stopping, augmentation, architecture, and seeded repetitions control or measure generalization without tuning on the test.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: Three seeded runs compare a baseline with weight decay using the same validation policy, report mean and range, and evaluate the frozen choice once on test. Your goal is to design and compare a controlled neural-network experiment without test-set leakage. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Supervised learning; Data and experimental workflow; Loss, gradients and optimization; Train, validation, test and leakage. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: Neural experiments separate training, validation, and test evidence; regularization, early stopping, augmentation, architecture, and seeded repetitions control or measure generalization without tuning on the test. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: Seeds do not remove nondeterminism, early stopping uses validation repeatedly, augmentation can change labels, regularization can underfit, hardware/library behavior varies, and one run hides variability. The learner must reject this overclaim: Setting one random seed makes neural training exactly reproducible and checking the test after every epoch is harmless monitoring.

includes: Use validation, early stopping, regularization and seeded experiments while reporting variability.
Design and compare a controlled neural-network experiment without test-set leakage.
excludes: Seeds do not remove nondeterminism, early stopping uses validation repeatedly, augmentation can change labels, regularization can underfit, hardware/library behavior varies, and one run hides variability.

outcomes: Design and compare a controlled neural-network experiment without test-set leakage.

examples.title: Validation, regularization and reproducibility: bounded direct case
examples.situation: Three seeded runs compare a baseline with weight decay using the same validation policy, report mean and range, and evaluate the frozen choice once on test.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: Three seeded runs compare a baseline with weight decay using the same validation policy, report mean and range, and evaluate the frozen choice once on test.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Record environment and seeds, repeat controlled runs, use validation for choices, reserve test for the frozen procedure, and report remaining variability.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Record environment and seeds, repeat controlled runs, use validation for choices, reserve test for the frozen procedure, and report remaining variability.

examples.title: Validation, regularization and reproducibility: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: Test accuracy is not monitored each epoch because that would adapt model selection to the held-out set.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: Test accuracy is not monitored each epoch because that would adapt model selection to the held-out set.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: Seeds do not remove nondeterminism, early stopping uses validation repeatedly, augmentation can change labels, regularization can underfit, hardware/library behavior varies, and one run hides variability.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: Setting one random seed makes neural training exactly reproducible and checking the test after every epoch is harmless monitoring.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Design and compare a controlled neural-network experiment without test-set leakage.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: Setting one random seed makes neural training exactly reproducible and checking the test after every epoch is harmless monitoring.
misconceptions.correction: Record environment and seeds, repeat controlled runs, use validation for choices, reserve test for the frozen procedure, and report remaining variability.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: Setting one random seed makes neural training exactly reproducible and checking the test after every epoch is harmless monitoring.

analogy.example: a layered numeric circuit whose weights, biases, activations, loss, and updates can be traced on a tiny example can introduce Validation, regularization and reproducibility only after its canonical technical definition and evidence boundary are stated.
usefulFor: forward passes, gradient updates, regularization evidence, and architectural roles
limitations: a toy circuit does not explain large-network representations, training data, emergent behavior, or deployment safety

recap.summary: You should now be able to design and compare a controlled neural-network experiment without test-set leakage. Keep this boundary in view: Seeds do not remove nondeterminism, early stopping uses validation repeatedly, augmentation can change labels, regularization can underfit, hardware/library behavior varies, and one run hides variability.
